evals.prompt: I'm building a dark-themed map for a nighttime food delivery app. The map needs to show restaurants, delivery routes, and driver locations clearly. What colors and visual hierarchy should I use?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend a dark base (near-black background, dark gray roads), with high-contrast accent colors for key elements (bright colors for restaurants and drivers), and lower-contrast muted colors for background infrastructure. Should mention contrast ratios for accessibility.
expectations: Recommends a dark background color (e.g., near-black or very dark gray)
Addresses visual hierarchy: drivers/restaurants should stand out most
Recommends high-contrast, bright colors for key interactive elements (restaurants, driver pins)
Mentions WCAG contrast ratio requirements or accessibility considerations
Provides specific color recommendations (hex values or color ranges) rather than only abstract guidance

evals.prompt: I'm designing a choropleth map showing population density across US counties. The data ranges from near 0 to 70,000 people per square mile (heavily skewed — most counties are low density, but a few urban counties are extremely high). What color scale and data classification approach do you recommend?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend a logarithmic or quantile classification to handle the skewed distribution, not equal-interval. Should recommend a sequential color scheme for a single-variable map. Should warn against equal-interval which would make most of the map look the same.
expectations: Recommends quantile, jenks/natural breaks, or logarithmic classification for skewed data
Warns against equal-interval classification for heavily skewed data
Recommends a sequential (single-hue) color scheme for density data
Addresses the number of classes to use (typically 5-7)
Mentions colorblind-safe palette options or accessibility

evals.prompt: My map has road labels, city names, neighborhood labels, and street address text. At zoom level 12 (neighborhood level), everything is showing and it's visually overwhelming. How should I approach text hierarchy and zoom-based label management?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend a clear typographic hierarchy with size and weight differentiation, zoom-level filtering (showing only relevant labels at each zoom), and label collision detection. Should give specific guidance on text sizes and when each label type should appear.
expectations: Recommends differentiated text sizes and/or font weights for different label types
Suggests zoom-based filtering: different label categories appear at different zoom levels
Provides a recommended hierarchy (e.g., city > neighborhood > street > address)
Gives specific size recommendations or zoom level thresholds
